entries.title: Ephraim the Syrian on Genesis 18
entries.excerpt: Since the Giver, God, had appointed that in the next year the gift would be sent down to Abraham, Abraham was considering whether or not the time would be revealed to him at which he should receive the blessing and Sarah’s closed womb should be opened. While Abraham was thinking on this, “the Lord… appeared to him… as he sat at the door of his tent in the heat of the day” (Gen 18:1). Abraham wished to satisfy the eyes of his soul with this revelation, but God became invisible to him.…
